official_name: Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik
notes: German federal cybersecurity agency. **NOTE: distinct from British Standards Institution (`bsigroup.com`)** — different country, different organization. BSI Germany publishes IT-Grundschutz (the German national IT-security framework — FREE download), the BSI Technical Guidelines (TR series), and the German eID infrastructure specs.

data.notes: FREE download (PDF + Kompendium HTML) with BSI Germany copyright. The dominant national-cybersecurity framework in Germany. Aligned with ISO/IEC 27001. Mostly in German; partial English translations.
Free download; copyright retained. Notable TRs: TR-03124 (eID-Client), TR-03127 (Authentication Service for eID-Card), TR-03130 (eID-Server), TR-03159 (Mobile Identity Documents).
Free technical specs via BSI TRs.

Baseline security catalogue for cloud service providers, used as the basis for independent C5 attestation audits. Free download; copyright retained by BSI. Domain code IDM (Identity and Access Management) in C5:2020 was renamed IAM in C5:2026, which also added a new GC domain ("General Conditions" — CSP transparency/contractual disclosures, e.g. applicable law and recovery parameters; lives on its own "C5_Conditions" tab in the editable workbook, distinct from the audited-criteria "C5_Criteria" tab, but still addressed as GC-NN) and expanded several existing domains (e.g. CRY grew from 4 to 19 criteria). Domain examples for 2026 verified directly against BSI's official editable workbook (C5_2026_Editable_en.xlsx); confirmed BCM (Business Continuity Management) is correct — an earlier draft mistakenly carried a "BC" domain from a PDF text-extraction artifact, and "BCS" does not appear anywhere in the source. 2020 domain/criterion IDs verified only against extracted PDF text, not the 2020 editable workbook. General Conditions evolved across versions: C5:2020 numbered them BC-01…BC-06 on a separate sheet, whereas C5:2026 promoted General Conditions into the audited GC domain (GC-NN).

versions_available.note: 168 criteria across 18 domains (AM, BCM, COM, COS, CRY, DEV, GC, HR, IAM, INQ, OIS, OPS, PI, PS, PSS, SIM, SP, SSO). Second substantive revision.
121 criteria. Domain set includes IDM (later renamed IAM in 2026) and no GC domain. Most existing third-party cross-mappings (ISO/IEC 27001, CSA CCM) target this version.

children.description: Control domain (e.g., OIS, CRY, IAM/IDM, OPS, GC). Contains multiple criteria.
Specific criterion (e.g., OIS-01, CRY-05, OPS-24, GC-01).

Supplementary criterion under a parent criterion, tagged Basic (B) or Additional (A) evidence — e.g. BCM-01.01B, AM-09.02A. Confirmed present in C5:2026's official editable workbook; not yet confirmed whether C5:2020 uses this same sub-numbering scheme.

match_nodes.description: AI Cloud Computing Compliance Criteria Catalogue (AIC4)

data.official_name: AI Cloud Computing Compliance Criteria Catalogue (AIC4)
data.notes: BSI's AI-specific companion to C5: attestation criteria for the security and trustworthiness of AI-based cloud services, attested under ISAE 3000 like C5. 40 audited criteria across 8 areas — PC (Preliminary Criteria), SR (Security & Robustness), PF (Performance & Functionality), RE (Reliability), DQ (Data Quality), DM (Data Management), EX (Explainability), BI (Bias). A separate General Conditions dimension (BC-01…BC-06) describes the AI service context rather than audited controls; BC-NN IDs share the DOMAIN-NN format. Area/BC codes and criterion format verified against BSI's official editable AIC4 workbook.

versions_available.note: First and current edition — 40 criteria across 8 areas (BI, DM, DQ, EX, PC, PF, RE, SR).

children.description: Criteria area (PC, SR, PF, RE, DQ, DM, EX, BI) or the General Conditions series (BC).
Specific criterion (e.g., SR-01, DQ-03, EX-02, BI-01), or General Conditions item (BC-01…BC-06).
